channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Ministry of Defence Admits to Past Human Radiation Experiments on Test Subjects
item.description: The UK Ministry of Defence has formally acknowledged conducting extensive human radiation experiments on military personnel and civilians during the Cold War era. These revelations, disclosed in a rec...

item.title: DCI Unveils Breakthrough in Investigation of TUK Lecturer's Murder
item.description: Detectives from Kenya's Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have arrested Richard Gikuhe Mbugua, a key suspect in the murder of Edgar Mokua, a lecturer at Technical University of Kenya (TUK)....

item.title: Elvis Chipezeze commits to helping Magesi return to Premiership after relegation
item.description: Magesi goalkeeper Elvis Chipezeze has expressed his commitment to staying with the club as they navigate life in the Motsepe Foundation Championship (MFC) following their relegation from the Betway Pr...

item.title: Public Engagement Crucial for Impeachment Trial Success
item.description: The article discusses Franklin Drilon's emphasis on the importance of public engagement and civil society's role in ensuring accountability during the Sara Duterte impeachment trial. Drilon, a veteran...

item.title: Security forces kill 10 militants and arrest two female suicide bombers during intelligence-based operations in Balochistan
item.description: Pakistan's security forces carried out two intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in the Mastung and Surab districts of Balochistan over the past 48 hours, during which 10 militants were killed and two ...

item.title: Drones Target US Consulate in Iraq Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: A drone attack near the U.S. consulate in Erbil, Iraq, has raised concerns about regional security and diplomatic safety. Reports indicate that drones struck the area surrounding the diplomatic facili...

item.title: Tamara Lich Seeks Court Approval for International Travel, Including White House Visit
item.description: Tamara Lich, a key figure in the 2022 Ottawa convoy protest that paralyzed downtown for nearly a month, is requesting court permission to travel internationally, including visiting the White House and...

item.title: UDOM Lecturer Released on Bail After 13 Days in Custody
item.description: The High Court of Tanzania has ordered the release of Melkisedeki Kitunzi Kaijage, a University of Dodoma (UDOM) lecturer, on bail after 13 days in custody. The ruling came as investigations into sedi...

item.title: Russia's false claim of capturing Kostyantynivka highlights prolonged conflict in Ukraine
item.description: The article details Russia's alleged capture of Kostyantynivka, a town in eastern Ukraine, and the subsequent military response by Ukrainian forces. Despite Russian claims of control, satellite imager...

item.title: Oluremi Tinubu Promotes Akwete Fabric, Announces ₦2 Billion Support for Indigenous Textile Industry
item.description: President Bola Tinubu's wife, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, has pledged to wear the traditional Akwete fabric during her husband's second term inauguration if he's re-elected. During her visit to the Akwete...

item.title: Pakistan reaffirms support for Saudi Arabia and warns against threats to Red Sea maritime trade
item.description: Pakistan has strongly condemned the continued threats issued by the Houthis against Saudi Arabia and commercial shipping operating in the Red Sea, describing such actions as a serious risk to regional...

item.title: Caffeine Consumption and Heart Health: Guidelines for Safe Intake
item.description: A recent scientific statement from the American Heart Association highlights that consuming up to 400 mg of caffeine per day (approximately five 8-ounce cups of coffee) is generally safe for most adul...

item.title: Tech company ordered to pay £2.5m in damages after court rules in favour of ex-employee
item.description: A UK court has ruled in favour of a former employee who successfully sued a major tech company for unfair dismissal and workplace harassment. The landmark ruling, which awarded £2.5 million in damages...

item.title: Kalonzo Musyoka Launches Fundraising Campaign for 2027 Presidential Bid
item.description: Wiper Patriotic Front leader Kalonzo Musyoka has launched an M-Pesa paybill and online portal to collect donations for his 2027 presidential campaign. The initiative, announced via the party's Faceboo...

item.title: Internal Labor Party Conflict Over Israel and Hamas Platform Stance
item.description: The head of Australia's peak Jewish body, Peter Wertheim, has criticized the Labor Party for omitting a call for Hamas to disarm from its draft policy platform, accusing the party of lacking balance. ...

item.title: Jewish group criticizes Labor's platform for omitting key Hamas and Palestinian commitments
item.description: The head of Australia's peak Jewish body, Peter Wertheim, has condemned the Australian Labor Party (ALP) for removing references to Hamas disarming from its draft policy platform. Wertheim, a longtime...

item.title: KMTC Opens September 2026 Intake Applications with August 11 Deadline
item.description: The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) has opened applications for its September 2026 intake, with a deadline set for August 11, 2026. Prospective students can apply for pre-service programmes thro...

item.title: Saskatchewan Roughriders to honour Bhishma Rajyaguru after fatal crash
item.description: The Saskatchewan Roughriders will honour Bhishma Rajyaguru, a 22-year-old international student from Gujarat, India, who died in a fatal collision with Roughriders linebacker Jayden Dalke on Highway 1...

item.title: UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham Struggles to Revive Sluggish Economy Amid Fiscal Pressures
item.description: The article discusses the economic challenges faced by Andy Burnham, the newly appointed UK Prime Minister, as he attempts to stimulate a sluggish economy. Burnham, from the Labour Party, must balance...

item.title: House prosecution ends Matibag's testimony without redirect examination in Sara Duterte impeachment trial
item.description: The House prosecution panel announced that it will no longer conduct a redirect examination of National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag, who served as its final witness for the a...
